rows.description: Dec: 28 to Francis King his Bill
rows.notes: Clear handwriting and alignment. Pounds/shillings/pence columns unambiguous; pence shows a half-penny symbol (interpreted as 0.5).

rows.description: to ye Dean of Ch. Ch. sent for being Venison
rows.notes: Description legible though slightly cramped. Amount reads clearly as 00 02 06 in the aligned columns.
Very clear. Columns and numerals sharp; 1 shilling recorded.
Clear alignment and numerals: 01 00 00.
Clear figures (08 13 06). Column boundaries unambiguous.

rows.description: 17 to Mr Weston four years Interest Due at Xmas 1743
rows.notes: Large pound figure (128) very clear; shillings and pence zero.

rows.description: to Mr Weston upon bond by common consent
rows.notes: Clear reading of 32 00 00; column lines well aligned.

rows.description: to Mr Blos one Rheam of Imp. Paper
rows.notes: Description legible though slightly abbreviated. Amount reads as 02 12 06; digits clear but handwriting a bit cursive.
Amount appears as 16 05 02; alignment consistent though ink faint in places.
Numbers read as 13 17 00; description clear. Minor ink flourish on the last zero but unambiguous.
Clear entry and numerals (41 08 04).

rows.description: May 19 to set wine to entertain Mr Wilmot in Ck
rows.notes: 'set' in the description is struck through on the page; included here exactly and noted as struck-through. Amount reads clearly as 00 01 06.

rows.description: June 12 to Mr Boylis Works for ye Library
rows.notes: Clear: 05 05 00 and wording readable.

rows.description: July 7 to Mrs Combes her Bill
rows.notes: Clear numerals (02 04 00).

rows.description: 10 to Mr Will. Talbot his Plate money
rows.notes: Reads 05 00 00; the handwriting for 'Will.' is slightly abbreviated but amount is clear.

rows.description: Aug 4 to Mr Townsend his Bill
rows.notes: Pence shown with a three-quarter symbol (interpreted as 0.75). Figures read as 44 05 11 3/4; slight ink flourish but columns align.

rows.description: 10 to Landtax for ye year 1742. & 1743. 4l. Aid
rows.notes: Description contains abbreviations; amount reads 14 08 00 clearly in aligned columns.

rows.description: 20 brought to acct wch was pd Newman for Wine
rows.notes: This line is a carried/bring-forward style line and has no amount in the right columns. Words 'brought to acct' and 'Newman for Wine' are legible but the line breaks into a following line that carries the numeric amount.
Continuation of previous line; amount appears as 00 03 00 aligned with this text. Handwriting slightly cramped but numbers clear.

rows.description: 28 to Salisbury Carrier for 2 Boxes from Mr Crabb
rows.notes: Description slightly abbreviated but legible; amount reads 00 09 04 in aligned columns.

rows.description: Oct. 10 to Mr Horner for Paper Ink &c.
rows.notes: Amount reads 00 17 04; 'Ink &c.' abbreviated but clear in context.

rows.description: to Sedgley for binding two Buttry Books
rows.notes: Amount 01 04 00 clear; description legible though 'Buttry' is an older spelling.
Shillings show 18 (00 18 00) — clearly within valid 0–19 range. Strong ink and alignment.

rows.description: 20 to Bayliffe for carrying away Rubbish
rows.notes: Amount reads 02 03 00; description and numerals clear.
First subtotal in the right-hand totals column reads 386 12 06 1/4. Alignment with ruling lines is clear; three-column reading unambiguous.
This subtotal originally has a '9' crossed out and a '7' written over it in the pounds column. The final intended pounds value is 177 (originally 179). Shillings/pence read clearly as 15 02.
Third subtotal reads 90 07 05 1/4. Columns and fraction legible and consistent with other totals.
Fourth subtotal appears as 94 05 05 with a small fractional mark (interpreted as 1/4). The fraction is faint; chosen 0.25 as most consistent with nearby subtotal fractions.
Central boxed sum reads 751 01 06 3/4. The boxed value is clear and aligns with the column totals above; some abbreviation in the label but numeric reading clear.
Latin phrasing (per soluti quæ erant) followed by the same figure 751 01 06 3/4. Text is a little abbreviated; numbers align with the boxed sum above.
This deduction line reads 739 02 09 clearly; phrase 'Deductis receptis quæ erant' is legible enough to indicate subtraction of receipts.

Balance line reads as 11.18.00¾ (11 pounds, 18 shillings, 0 pence + 3/4). On the page the '00' in the pence column was originally written then struck through and '18' written over it in the adjacent column area (per page guidance). The final intended reading is 11 pounds, 18 shillings, 00 pence + 3/4. The crossing/overwriting required careful alignment checking against ruling lines, hence moderate confidence.
Signature/name line in Latinized form; handwriting is somewhat stylized. No monetary amount. Reading 'Dogcumbe' vs 'Documbe' uncertain; chosen 'Dogcumbe' based on letter shapes.
Signature line; likely 'Francis Webber Jun. Rector' or 'Francis Webber Sub Rector' — letters are abbreviated. No amounts; moderate uncertainty in exact honorific.
Name and degree abbreviated; the surname reading 'Gosnold' is plausible but not certain. Ink is lighter here; no monetary data.
Abbreviated given name and initials 'Car: Webber A.M.' — likely 'Car.' for 'Carolus' or 'Charles'. Signature-style writing makes precise reading moderate confidence.
Short signature line; letters are unclear (interpreted as 'Gul: Keffner' but uncertain). No amounts present.
Final signature line; 'Tho: Bray' is a plausible reading but the degree abbreviation is faint. No monetary data.
